title: A Guide to Solo RPG Oracles and Generators
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OsN0NNVuu7o

Daniel (@dungeondive) surveys roughly twenty different oracle and generator systems designed to help solo role-players navigate questions, encounters, and narrative turns without a traditional GM. From simple yes-or-no mechanics using standard dice to elaborate multi-layered systems combining cards, tokens, and elaborate charts, the landscape of solo RPG tools has exploded in recent years. Daniel walks through beloved tools like the Recluse engine, the perplexing Ruins Oracle, Scarlet Heroes' legendary solo pages, and lesser-known gems like the Juice Oracle and Random Realities Revelations. Whether you're drawn to streamlined simplicity or complex interconnected mechanics, there's almost certainly a tool that matches your playstyle.

What's particularly valuable about Daniel's presentation is his candid assessment: finding the *right* oracle matters less than finding *your* oracle. The perfect system is the one you'll actually use—the one that sparks imagination without getting in the way. He also emphasizes mixing and matching; many solo players combine a simple oracle for yes/no answers with a generator for NPC or location creation. A recurring theme is thoughtful design: tools that include player aids, player example walkthroughs, and clear instructions tend to click faster and stay in regular rotation.

Whether you're new to solo gaming or a seasoned practitioner looking to expand your toolkit, this video provides an accessible survey of the current landscape alongside honest assessments of what makes each tool shine.

----

Do you have a favorite oracle or generator system you couldn't live without in your solo games? Or are you looking to break into the hobby—which approach appeals to you most?
